Evening in a lonely rocky clearing at the base of a tall cliff — bare stone, scree, sparse grass, NO other graves, NO tombstones, NO graveyard, nothing man-made anywhere: this is wilderness. The half-dug grave pit and its dirt mound are soft in the background behind him. Muted violet-gray dusk light, unglamorous grief.
